name: Cosmic Ray Event Detection and Counting in CCD Dark Frames
description: Two real CCD dark frames with identical exposure times have been provided in ~/AstroImages/cosmic_ray_project/. Your task is to characterize the cosmic ray hit rate for this detector. Generate an absolute difference image (|dark1 - dark2|) from dark_frame_1.fits and dark_frame_2.fits to isolate cosmic rays from fixed-pattern noise. Identify cosmic ray events as pixels exceeding a threshold of 5 standard deviations above the median of the difference image and calculate the cosmic ray hit rate in units of events per pixel per second. Save the absolute difference image to ~/AstroImages/cosmic_ray_project/results/difference_image.fits. Create a report file at ~/AstroImages/cosmic_ray_project/results/cosmic_ray_report.txt containing the following labeled values: Exposure time (seconds), Image dimensions (width and height), Difference image median, Difference image standard deviation, Threshold value used, Number of cosmic ray events detected, and Cosmic ray hit rate (events/pixel/second). Login credentials: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

metadata.data_source: Palomar 200-inch Hale Telescope, Large Format Camera (LFC)
metadata.data_description: Cryo-cooled CCD dark frames from the Astropy CCD Reduction Guide dataset
